title: lysimachia brachyandra
intro: Lysimachia brachyandra, also known as the New Zealand joint-purslane or brachyandra, is a perennial herb native to New Zealand and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Lysimachia brachyandra include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Lysimachia brachyandra is often prepared as a poultice or infused oil for topical application, and it can also be made into a tea for internal use.
